results.rendered_html: liṅga—śarīra n. the subtle body which accompanies the individual spirit or soul in all its transmigrations and is not destroyed by death (it is also called sūkṣma-ś˚, q.v., and since it is the sign and accompaniment of individuality it can never perish till the individualized soul is finally merged in the Universal),  Kap. ;  Vedāntas.  ( IW. 53 n. 2 ).
